# Figure description: Figure_13.png

**Source**: `usgs_teniz_copper_bundle/descriptions/Figure_13.png` (PDF page 35)

---

## Figure Type
This is a histogram showing the probability distribution of Monte Carlo simulation results for undiscovered mineral resources.

## Coverage and Subject
The figure presents Monte Carlo simulation results for undiscovered sandstone copper resources in tract 142ssCu8050 (TZ-1), Teniz Basin, Kazakhstan. The histogram displays the distribution of contained copper estimates across different probability levels (Mean, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 0.9, and 0.95 probabilities).

## Key Content
The histogram displays blue horizontal bars representing different probability levels on the y-axis against contained copper quantities (in million metric tons) on the x-axis. The x-axis extends from 0 to 40 million metric tons. The bars show:
- Mean probability: extends to approximately 5 million metric tons
- 0.05 probability: extends to approximately 30 million metric tons (the longest bar)
- 0.1 probability: extends to approximately 25 million metric tons
- 0.5 probability: very short bar, close to zero
- 0.9 probability: very short bar, minimal extent
- 0.95 probability: minimal to no visible extent

The distribution shows a right-skewed pattern typical of mineral resource assessments, with higher probabilities (0.9, 0.95) associated with smaller copper quantities and lower probabilities (0.05, 0.1) associated with larger potential resources.

## Text and Labels
**Title (caption):** "Figure 13. Histogram showing the results of a Monte Carlo computer simulation of undiscovered sandstone copper resources in tract 142ssCu8050 (TZ-1), Teniz Basin, Kazakhstan."

**Y-axis label:** "Probability"

**X-axis label:** "Contained copper, in million metric tons"

**Y-axis values:** Mean, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 0.9, 0.95

**X-axis scale:** 0, 10, 20, 30, 40

**Page header:** "Estimate of the Number of Undiscovered Deposits    27"

## Research Value
This figure provides critical probabilistic estimates for copper resource potential in the Teniz Basin permissive tract, showing the range of possible undiscovered copper quantities at different confidence levels. The histogram reveals that there is a 5% probability (0.05 level) of discovering up to 30 million metric tons of contained copper, while the mean estimate is approximately 5 million metric tons, helping geologists and resource planners understand both the expected value and the uncertainty range of potential copper resources in this assessment tract.
